events.name: UW Huskies Baseball 2026 Season
events.description: University of Washington Huskies baseball at Husky Ballpark. Catch Big Ten conference games and non-conference matchups throughout the spring season.

events.name: UW Cherry Blossom Festival
events.description: The University of Washington Quad transforms into a sea of pink as 30 Yoshino cherry trees bloom. A beloved Seattle spring tradition with picnics, photos, and cultural celebrations.

events.name: First Thursday Art Walk
events.description: Pioneer Square's monthly art walk. Galleries open their doors, artists show new work, and the neighborhood comes alive with free receptions, live music, and street performers.

events.name: Seattle International Film Festival
events.description: One of the largest and most acclaimed film festivals in North America. 25 days of premieres, documentaries, shorts, and filmmaker Q&As across multiple venues.

events.name: University District Street Fair
events.description: The longest-running street fair in the Northwest. Ten blocks of artists, crafters, food vendors, live music, and community booths along University Way.

events.name: Northwest Folklife
events.description: The nation's largest free community-powered arts festival. Four days of music, dance, crafts, and food celebrating cultural traditions from around the world at Seattle Center.

events.name: Seattle Chamber Music Society Summer Festival
events.description: World-class chamber music across concert halls, islands, parks, and neighborhoods. Featuring James Ehnes, Inon Barnatan, Tessa Lark, and more. Three weeks of mainstage concerts, free community performances, and special events.

events.name: Seattle Juneteenth Celebration
events.description: Citywide celebration of freedom and Black culture with live music, art, food, speakers, and community gatherings at Judkins Park and across the Central District.

events.name: Fremont Solstice Parade & Fair
events.description: Fremont's legendary summer solstice celebration featuring the famous painted cyclists parade, quirky floats, live music, street performers, and a neighborhood street fair.

events.name: Capitol Hill Pride
events.description: A free, community-organized celebration of LGBTQ+ pride on Capitol Hill featuring a street festival, live performances, drag shows, and community organizations.

events.name: Seattle Pride
events.description: Seattle's annual LGBTQ+ pride celebration with parade, festival, and community events across Capitol Hill and downtown.

events.name: 4th of Jul-Ivar's Fireworks
events.description: Seattle's iconic Independence Day fireworks show over Lake Union, sponsored by Ivar's. Best viewed from Gas Works Park, Lake Union Park, or by boat on the lake.

events.name: KEXP Concerts at the Mural
events.description: Free outdoor concert series at Seattle Center's Mural Amphitheatre, curated by KEXP. Featuring local and touring artists every weekend through the summer.

events.name: Timber! Outdoor Music Festival
events.description: An intimate outdoor music festival set in the woods of Carnation, WA. Two days of indie, folk, and experimental music with camping, swimming, and nature.

events.name: Ballard SeafoodFest
events.description: Annual neighborhood seafood festival featuring fresh salmon, crab, and oysters from PNW waters, plus live music on multiple stages, a beer garden, and a lutefisk eating contest.

events.name: West Seattle Summer Fest
events.description: A neighborhood street festival along the West Seattle Junction featuring live music, a beer garden, arts and crafts, kids activities, and over 100 vendor booths.

events.name: Bite of Seattle
events.description: The Pacific Northwest's largest food festival featuring 60+ restaurants, live cooking demos, and entertainment.

events.name: CID Dragon Fest
events.description: Chinatown-International District's annual summer celebration with dragon and lion dances, martial arts demonstrations, live performances, and food from across Asia.

events.name: Seafair
events.description: Seattle's signature summer festival featuring the Blue Angels air show, hydroplane races on Lake Washington, a torchlight parade, and community events across the city.

events.name: Capitol Hill Block Party
events.description: Seattle's premier indie music festival taking over the streets of Capitol Hill with multiple stages, local food vendors, and art installations.

events.name: Emerald City Comic Con
events.description: The Pacific Northwest's largest comic and pop culture convention. Celebrity panels, artist alley, cosplay contests, exclusive merch, and exhibitor halls at the Washington State Convention Center.

events.name: Bumbershoot
events.description: Seattle's premier music and arts festival at Seattle Center. Three days of live music, comedy, film, visual arts, and more.

events.name: PAX West
events.description: The premier gaming convention on the West Coast. Four days of playable demos, panels with game developers, esports tournaments, concerts, and tabletop gaming at the Washington State Convention Center.

events.name: UW Huskies Football 2026 Season
events.description: University of Washington Huskies football at Husky Stadium. One of the most iconic college football settings in the country, with views of Lake Washington and the Cascades.

events.name: Fremont Oktoberfest
events.description: Seattle's biggest Oktoberfest celebration in the heart of Fremont. Three days of craft beer, bratwurst, live music, stein hoisting, and keg bowling.

events.name: Earshot Jazz Festival
events.description: Seattle's premier jazz festival presenting world-class musicians across multiple venues over three weeks. From avant-garde to swing, bebop to fusion.

events.name: Día de los Muertos Seattle
events.description: Seattle's vibrant Day of the Dead celebration featuring altars (ofrendas), traditional music, Aztec dance, face painting, food, and a community procession.

events.name: Winterfest at Seattle Center
events.description: Seattle Center's annual holiday celebration with an ice rink, winter train rides, a holiday market, live performances, and the sparkling Winterfest lights.

stages.name: Vashon Island Center for the Arts

scheduleItems.title: Rachmaninoff & Mendelssohn
scheduleItems.description: Rachmaninoff: Symphonic Dances for Two Pianos, Op. 45. Mendelssohn: Octet in E-flat Major, Op. 20.

scheduleItems.title: Ravaei World Premiere, Mendelssohn & Brahms
scheduleItems.description: Kian Ravaei: A Free People (co-commissioned, world premiere). Mendelssohn: Piano Trio No. 1 in D Minor. Brahms: Piano Quartet No. 3 in C Minor.

scheduleItems.title: Haydn, Respighi & Dvořák
scheduleItems.description: Haydn: Piano Trio in A-flat Major. Respighi: Piano Quintet in F Minor. Dvořák: String Quartet No. 12 "American".

scheduleItems.title: Beethoven, Dohnányi & Dvořák
scheduleItems.description: Beethoven: String Quartet, Op. 18 No. 1. Dohnányi: Serenade for String Trio. Dvořák: String Quartet No. 12 "American".

scheduleItems.title: Beethoven, Dohnányi & Dvořák
scheduleItems.description: Beethoven: String Quartet, Op. 18 No. 1. Dohnányi: Serenade for String Trio. Dvořák: String Quartet No. 12 "American".

scheduleItems.title: Beethoven, Barber & Suk
scheduleItems.description: Beethoven: String Trio, Op. 9 No. 1. Barber: String Quartet. Suk: Piano Quartet, Op. 1.

scheduleItems.title: Amy Beach, Brahms & Mendelssohn
scheduleItems.description: Amy Beach: Piano Trio. Brahms: Violin Sonata No. 1 in G Major. Mendelssohn: String Quintet No. 1 in A Major.

scheduleItems.title: Free Community Concert
scheduleItems.description: Dvořák: Terzetto in C Major, Op. 74. Suk: Piano Quartet in A Minor, Op. 1. Free outdoor concert at Volunteer Park.

scheduleItems.title: Bansal World Premiere, Fauré & Dvořák
scheduleItems.description: Juhi Bansal: New Work (SCMS commission, world premiere). Fauré: Violin Sonata No. 1 in A Major. Dvořák: Piano Quartet No. 2 in E-flat Major.

scheduleItems.title: Shostakovich, Walker & Brahms
scheduleItems.description: Shostakovich: Piano Trio No. 1. George Walker: String Quartet No. 1. Brahms: Piano Quartet No. 1 in G Minor.

scheduleItems.title: Piazzolla, Adams & Schubert
scheduleItems.description: Piazzolla: Histoire du Tango. John Adams: Shaker Loops for String Septet. Schubert: Piano Trio No. 1 in B-flat Major.

scheduleItems.title: Festival Finale: Shostakovich, Grieg & Copland
scheduleItems.description: Shostakovich: Two Pieces for String Octet. Grieg: Sonata for Cello and Piano. Copland: Appalachian Spring (original 13-instrument chamber version).

scheduleItems.title: Concert: MC Frontalot
scheduleItems.description: Nerdcore hip-hop live
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Pioneer of nerdcore hip-hop. Rapping about technology, gaming, and geek culture since 2000. A PAX mainstay and self-proclaimed world champion of nerdcore.

scheduleItems.title: Whitney Ballen
scheduleItems.description: Seattle singer-songwriter
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Seattle singer-songwriter blending folk and indie rock with intimate, emotionally raw storytelling and fingerpicked guitar.

scheduleItems.title: Enumclaw
scheduleItems.description: Tacoma-based indie rock
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Tacoma-based indie rock band channeling 90s alternative with heavy guitars and anthemic hooks. Named after the small Washington town.

scheduleItems.title: Shabazz Palaces
scheduleItems.description: Experimental hip-hop from Seattle
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Seattle experimental hip-hop duo led by Ishmael Butler (formerly of Digable Planets). Known for avant-garde production, cosmic themes, and boundary-pushing live shows.

scheduleItems.title: La Luz
scheduleItems.description: Surf rock and dream pop
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Seattle-born surf rock and dream pop band led by Shana Cleveland. Lush harmonies, shimmering guitars, and a laid-back Pacific Northwest vibe.

scheduleItems.title: Thee Sacred Souls
scheduleItems.description: Soul and R&B
scheduleItems.performer_bio: San Diego soul trio crafting vintage-inspired R&B with silky vocals and deep grooves. Their self-titled debut earned widespread acclaim.

scheduleItems.title: Black Belt Eagle Scout
scheduleItems.description: Indie rock from Portland
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Katherine Paul is a Native American indie rock artist from Portland. Her music weaves dreamy shoegaze with themes of Indigenous identity and queer love.

scheduleItems.title: Faye Webster
scheduleItems.description: Indie pop and R&B
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Atlanta-based singer-songwriter blending indie pop, country, and R&B with dreamy pedal steel guitar and deadpan humor. Known for I Know I am Funny haha.

scheduleItems.title: Opening Ceremony
scheduleItems.description: Kick off Seafair 2026

scheduleItems.title: Torchlight Parade
scheduleItems.description: Annual parade through downtown Seattle

scheduleItems.title: Blue Angels Practice
scheduleItems.description: Friday practice flight over Lake Washington

scheduleItems.title: Live Music — Local Bands
scheduleItems.description: Local Seattle bands perform lakeside

scheduleItems.description: H1 Unlimited qualifying heats

scheduleItems.description: The main event — full aerobatic performance

scheduleItems.title: Hydroplane Finals
scheduleItems.description: H1 Unlimited championship final

scheduleItems.title: Blue Angels Air Show Day 2
scheduleItems.description: Sunday showcase over Lake Washington

scheduleItems.title: Local Openers
scheduleItems.description: Seattle emerging artists showcase

scheduleItems.title: Japanese Breakfast
scheduleItems.description: Indie pop headliner
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Japanese Breakfast is the moniker of Korean-American musician Michelle Zauner. Known for dreamy indie pop with shoegaze textures, her music explores themes of grief, identity, and joy. Her memoir Crying in H Mart became a New York Times bestseller.

scheduleItems.title: Thundercat
scheduleItems.description: Bass-forward funk and jazz
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Stephen Lee Bruner, known as Thundercat, is a Grammy-winning bassist, singer, and songwriter from Los Angeles. His virtuosic bass playing blends funk, jazz, and electronic music. Known for albums like Drunk and It Is What It Is.

scheduleItems.title: DJ Set — Late Night
scheduleItems.description: Electronic and dance music

scheduleItems.title: Comedy Hour
scheduleItems.description: Stand-up comedy between sets

scheduleItems.title: Courtney Barnett
scheduleItems.description: Australian indie rock
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Australian singer-songwriter and guitarist known for her witty, observational lyrics and raw indie rock sound. Her debut Sometimes I Sit and Think, and Sometimes I Just Sit was critically acclaimed worldwide.

scheduleItems.title: Bikini Kill
scheduleItems.description: Punk legends reunited
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Pioneering punk band from Olympia, WA, fronted by Kathleen Hanna. Central to the riot grrrl movement of the early 90s. Reunited in 2019 to massive acclaim.

scheduleItems.title: Opening Night Film
scheduleItems.description: World premiere kicks off SIFF 2026

scheduleItems.description: Curated selection of international documentaries

scheduleItems.description: Final screening and awards ceremony

scheduleItems.description: Dance party in Cal Anderson Park

scheduleItems.description: Local LGBTQ+ organizations, vendors, and resources

scheduleItems.title: Comedy Showcase
scheduleItems.description: Stand-up featuring Pacific Northwest comedians

scheduleItems.title: Local Natives
scheduleItems.description: Indie rock from Los Angeles
scheduleItems.performer_bio: LA-based indie rock band known for soaring harmonies and atmospheric arrangements. Albums like Gorilla Manor and Hummingbird defined a generation of indie music.

scheduleItems.title: The Head and the Heart
scheduleItems.description: Indie folk headliner from Seattle
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Seattle-formed indie folk band that rose from open mic nights at Ballard bar Conor Byrne. Known for heartfelt anthems like Rivers and Roads and All We Ever Knew.

scheduleItems.title: Indie Film Screening
scheduleItems.description: Curated short films from local filmmakers

scheduleItems.title: Macklemore
scheduleItems.description: Seattle hip-hop hometown show
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Seattle rapper and activist known for Thrift Shop, Same Love, and Can not Hold Us. Grammy winner, independent artist, and deeply rooted in the Pacific Northwest music scene.

scheduleItems.title: Brandi Carlile
scheduleItems.description: Grammy-winning singer-songwriter
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Grammy-winning singer-songwriter from Ravensdale, WA. Known for powerful vocals spanning folk, rock, and Americana. Her memoir Broken Horses and album In These Silent Days cemented her as a generational artist.

scheduleItems.title: Art Walk
scheduleItems.description: Guided tour of festival art installations

scheduleItems.title: Poetry Slam Finals
scheduleItems.description: Regional poetry slam championship

scheduleItems.title: Death Cab for Cutie
scheduleItems.description: Bellingham indie rock legends
scheduleItems.performer_bio: Bellingham-born indie rock institution led by Ben Gibbard. From Transatlanticism to Asphalt Meadows, they have defined Pacific Northwest indie rock for over two decades.
